title: Physics-Constrained Boosting Specialist
summary: Current best solutions already encode pit-window pressure, but the final learner is still free to fit non-physical reversals.
rationale: Current best solutions already encode pit-window pressure, but the final learner is still free to fit non-physical reversals. CatBoost supports monotone constraints, and F1 tyre-strategy literature treats degradation and pit timing as structurally directional processes with pit-stop resets. A small monotone specialist on the most causal pressure features could regularize sparse race/driver regimes better than another unconstrained feature expansion.
implementation_hint: Train a second model on a compact feature block such as `TyreLife`, `Laps_Remaining_Est`, `Service_Window_Pressure`, `Finish_Stop_Pressure`, `Cannot_Finish_On_Current_Tyre`, and degradation-rate features, with monotone signs chosen only where direction is defensible. Blend its OOF predictions with the main unconstrained model instead of replacing the main model.
expected_effect: More stable grouped CV and better leaderboard transfer from fewer overfit sign-flips in rare compounds, drivers, and late-race states.
risk: True effects are not globally monotone in wet/transition laps or near pit-window boundaries, so over-constraining too many raw features can hurt AUC.
